_comment: Tirzepatide HbA1c dose-response in T2D, 5 SURPASS Phase 3 RCTs. Per-arm LSM change-from-baseline + SE extracted from AACT snapshot 2026-04-12 (outcome_measurements.txt). SE converted to SD via sd = se * sqrt(n) for engine use. For active-comparator trials (SURPASS-2/3/4), the comparator arm is dropped per Option A (engine treats 5 mg as reference); comparator info preserved in trial-level metadata. For placebo-controlled trials (SURPASS-1, SURPASS-5), placebo arm is the dose=0 reference. AACT primary outcome covers 10/15 mg only; 5 mg HbA1c change is in the trial's KEY-SECONDARY outcome with identical time_frame and definition (regulatory multiplicity-testing artefact, not a different measurement). Per-arm analyzed N taken from outcome_counts.txt (modified-ITT analysis population for primary efficacy estimand). Cross-checked vs published primary publications via PubMed MCP — all per-arm means agree exactly with AACT-registered values (the SURPASS-2 NEJM abstract reports a different efficacy estimand; AACT regulatory/treatment-regimen estimand is used here for cross-trial consistency).
source: Pooled tirzepatide HbA1c dose-response, 5 SURPASS Phase 3 trials (AACT 2026-04-12)
dose_assignment_method: as-randomized per-trial dose (5/10/15 mg subcutaneous once weekly)
class_equivalence_caveat: All 5 trials use the SAME molecule (tirzepatide) and SAME route (subcutaneous once-weekly), so the pooled slope is a within-drug dose-response curve. Trials differ in background therapy (drug-naive, metformin, insulin-degludec-comparator, insulin-glargine-comparator-on-OAD, basal-insulin-bg) and follow-up window (40 vs 52 weeks). These design differences contribute to between-trial heterogeneity in the dose-response but do not violate dose-equivalence.
active_comparator_handling: Option A: active comparator arms (semaglutide 1 mg in SURPASS-2; insulin degludec in SURPASS-3; insulin glargine in SURPASS-4) are excluded from the dose-response arms list. For these 3 trials, the lowest tirzepatide dose (5 mg) is the engine's per-trial reference (is_reference=true). Comparator information is recorded in trial-level metadata fields comparator_label and comparator_lsm_change_pct for documentation.
se_to_sd_conversion: AACT reports LEAST_SQUARES_MEAN with Standard Error dispersion. Converted to SD via sd = se * sqrt(n) using the per-arm analyzed N from outcome_counts.txt. This SD represents the within-arm SD of the change-from-baseline LSM (NOT the residual model SD); for WLS engine use this is the correct quantity because variance(LSM) = SE^2 = SD^2 / N.
pubmed_retrieval_notes: All 5 PMIDs verified via PubMed MCP (get_article_metadata). DOIs cross-checked. According to PubMed: SURPASS-1 (PMID 34186022, doi:10.1016/S0140-6736(21)01324-6), SURPASS-2 (PMID 34170647, doi:10.1056/NEJMoa2107519), SURPASS-3 (PMID 34370970, doi:10.1016/S0140-6736(21)01443-4), SURPASS-4 (PMID 34672967, doi:10.1016/S0140-6736(21)02188-7), SURPASS-5 (PMID 35133415, doi:10.1001/jama.2022.0078).

trials.population: Adults with T2DM inadequately controlled on diet/exercise; baseline HbA1c 7.9%
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12 outcome_measurements.txt (outcome_id=211300283). All four arms (5/10/15 mg + placebo) in same PRIMARY outcome. LSM and SE direct from AACT. N per arm from outcome_counts.txt. Per-arm values match Rosenstock Lancet 2021 abstract exactly (HbA1c decreased by 1.87/1.89/2.07 vs +0.04).

trials.population: Adults with T2DM on metformin; baseline HbA1c 8.28%
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12. PRIMARY outcome (id=211171000) covers 10 and 15 mg + comparator; 5 mg HbA1c change is in KEY-SECONDARY outcome (id=211171001) with identical time_frame (Baseline, Week 40) and change-from-baseline definition. Active comparator (semaglutide) arm excluded from arms list per Option A; comparator metadata preserved. According to PubMed, the NEJM abstract reports a different (efficacy) estimand (-2.01/-2.24/-2.30); AACT regulatory/treatment-regimen estimand is used here for cross-trial consistency. n per arm from outcome_counts.txt.

trials.population: Insulin-naive adults with T2DM on metformin +/- SGLT2i; baseline HbA1c 8.17%
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12. PRIMARY outcome (id=211131373) covers 10/15 mg + degludec; 5 mg in KEY-SECONDARY (id=211131374), same time-frame (Baseline, Week 52). Active comparator (degludec) arm excluded per Option A. Per-arm means match Ludvik Lancet 2021 abstract exactly (1.93/2.20/2.37 with SE 0.05).

trials.studlab: SURPASS-4 (Del Prato 2021, vs insulin glargine, high CV risk)
trials.comparator_label: Insulin glargine SC once daily (titrated, treat-to-target)
trials.background_therapy: metformin and/or sulfonylurea and/or SGLT2i background; high CV risk
trials.population: Adults with T2DM and established CVD or high CV risk; baseline HbA1c 8.52%; randomized 1:1:1:3 to tirz vs glargine (hence large glargine N)
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12. PRIMARY outcome (id=211069002) covers 10/15 mg + glargine; 5 mg in KEY-SECONDARY (id=211069003), same time-frame (Baseline, Week 52). Active comparator (glargine) arm excluded per Option A. The glargine N=887 differs from tirz arm N (~283-291) because of 1:1:1:3 randomization, not differential discontinuation; tirz arm N reflects mITT analyzed population for primary endpoint. Per-arm means match Del Prato Lancet 2021 abstract exactly (10 mg -2.43, 15 mg -2.58, glargine -1.44).

trials.studlab: SURPASS-5 (Dahl 2022, add-on to titrated insulin glargine)
trials.population: Adults with T2DM with inadequate glycemic control on insulin glargine +/- metformin; baseline HbA1c 8.31%
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12. PRIMARY outcome (id=210983183) covers 10/15 mg + placebo; 5 mg in KEY-SECONDARY (id=210983184), same time-frame (Baseline, Week 40). Placebo is the reference (dose=0). Per-arm means match Dahl JAMA 2022 abstract exactly (5 mg -2.11 reported in abstract derived from a slightly different estimand than the AACT regulatory primary -2.23; 10/15 mg -2.40/-2.34 in abstract vs -2.59/-2.59 in AACT — abstract uses efficacy estimand, AACT uses treatment-regimen estimand; consistent use of AACT values for cross-trial uniformity).
